titleStmt.title: Letter from William Tyrrell Jr. to Elizabeth Tyrrell, 29 October 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Tyrrell Jr.

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
The letters of William Upton Tyrrell ('Willie') to his father William J.H. Tyrrell, mother Elizabeth, sister Minnie and brother Robert are a notable example of wartime correspondence. The letters - nearly 200 in total - are mostly written by William to his family while in active service with the Royal Irish Rifles in France. Willie discusses his life and the lives of other soldiers in the trenches, as well as life at Ballindoolin, the family estate.
The family name 'Tyrrell' is sometimes misspelled 'Tyrell' in correspondence to the family.
Summary of the present letter:
Letter from Willie to his mother.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
29 October 1917 My dear Mamy I was delighted to get letters from
you and Minnie yesterday. We are having a very easy time here
now. Practically nothing at all to
do. Of course when we charge my address
will be exactly the same as it is now
but I do not know when the charge
is taking place. The weather is much finer now but
cold. However we have a nice fire
in our mess room so don't care about
the cold at present. You may send
my muffler any time you like. I am sure Lewis will like being in the
snipe shooting section. Somehow I
think snipe would be rather hard to hit
with 18 pounders. However I don't know.
No news at all. Best love from Your mostt affec Son,
Willie
